title: The Soil That Reflects What It Has Forgotten

features.name: mirror-shards-settling
features.desc-on-touch: broken reflections lie half-buried in the loam—each shard shows not the sky above but the shapes the earth has absorbed and forgotten, images that have seeped into the soil and taken root there

features.name: root-remembering
features.desc-on-touch: a single eucalyptus root emerges from the brown soil, its surface carved with the grain pattern of something it once held—a mirror, a hand, a face—now part of the root's permanent memory, readable only by touch

features.name: sediment-rings
features.desc-on-touch: the soil reveals itself in concentric bands of deepening brown—each ring holds a different reflection the earth has learned to forget: colours fade to grey, grey fades to a darker absence, and at the very deepest band, something that was once a mirror now reads as just another layer of forgetting

features.name: groundwater-thinking
features.desc-on-touch: where water rises through the soil, it carries every reflection it learned underground—you cannot see your face here, only the shapes the darkness taught the water to hold

inscription: the deeper you go, the more the mirror remembers it was never glass

file.name: Soil_Cross_Section_Depth_Study.txt
file.note: layers of earth annotated with colours that have disappeared into the deepening, leaving only the darkening proof that something reflective once lay here

exits.label: back up to where mirrors still know what they reflect
deeper to where the soil's memory becomes unreadable
toward a field where earth is still learning its own reflection

reverie.title: REVERIE — soil becoming archive
narr: You knelt in the red earth where she was gathering
samples, your hands sinking deep. Every fistful held
the shape it had forgotten—a shard she had buried
years before. She said: the ground remembers better
than we do, and your fingers understood. The soil
learned your touch that morning and kept it.
